title: SkiaSharp Issue Triage
description: Per-issue AI triage output. Classification values ARE full GitHub labels (e.g., 'type/bug', 'os/Linux').

classifiedType.description: Issue type. Pick ONE best fit — trust issue content over title prefix.

classifiedArea.description: Component area. Pick MOST SPECIFIC match. Values are actual GitHub labels.

classifiedPlatform.description: Platform label. Omit platforms array entirely if not platform-specific.

classifiedBackend.description: Rendering backend label. Omit backends array entirely if not backend-specific.

classifiedTenet.description: Quality tenet label. Omit tenets array entirely if no tenets apply.

classifiedPartner.description: Third-party partner flag. Omit if no partner involvement.

suggestedAction.description: Recommended next action for the maintainer. needs-info=missing critical info from reporter. needs-reproduction=need a reliable minimal repro. needs-investigation=requires deeper code/runtime investigation. ready-to-fix=root cause understood, ready to implement. keep-open=valid issue, no immediate action. close-as-fixed=already resolved. close-as-duplicate=duplicate of another issue. close-as-not-a-bug=behavior is intentional/by-design. close-as-external=root cause is upstream Skia or another dependency.

actionRisk.description: low=reversible/cosmetic. medium=state change. high=irreversible or reputation-sensitive. For add-comment, risk is dynamic based on content type and confidence — see schema-cheatsheet.md.

analyzedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when triage was performed.

currentLabels.description: Labels currently on the issue at time of triage.

summary.description: Reporter-facing summary: what the user reported, their environment, and symptoms. Factual restatement only — no analysis or hypothesis.

classification.description: Issue classification labels and confidence scores.

platforms.description: Platform labels if issue is platform-specific, e.g. ['os/macOS', 'os/Linux']. Omit if not platform-specific.

backends.description: Rendering backend labels if backend-specific, e.g. ['backend/Metal']. Omit if not backend-specific.

tenets.description: Quality tenet labels, e.g. ['tenet/reliability']. Omit if no tenets apply.

partner.description: Third-party partner flag, e.g. 'partner/maui'. Omit if no partner involvement.

evidence.description: Evidence extracted from the issue: reproduction material, bug signals, version info, regression analysis, and fix status.

stepsToReproduce.description: Steps to reproduce as described by the reporter.

items.description: A code snippet from the issue.

codeSnippets.description: Code snippets from the issue body.

items.description: A file attachment from the issue.

environmentDetails.description: Reporter's environment as described in the issue.

relatedIssues.description: GitHub issue numbers referenced in the issue text.

items.description: A link to an external reproduction repository.

reproEvidence.description: Reproduction material extracted from the issue.

severity.description: Impact severity. critical=crash/data loss. high=feature broken. medium=degraded. low=cosmetic.

regressionClaimed.description: Quick flag: whether the reporter said this worked in a previous version. See evidence.regression for detailed analysis.

errorType.description: Category of error behavior. crash=segfault/SIGABRT/hard crash. exception=managed .NET exception thrown. memory-leak=growing memory or handles. build-error=compile/link/restore failure. wrong-output=produces incorrect results. missing-output=produces no output or empty. missing-api=requested API not yet exposed in SkiaSharp. performance=works but too slow. platform-specific=works on some platforms but not others. other=none of the above.

errorMessage.description: Primary error message from the report.

stackTrace.description: Stack trace if provided. Max 50 lines. Redact absolute paths.

reproQuality.description: How complete the reproduction steps are. complete=fully reproducible. partial=some steps missing. none=no repro steps.

bugSignals.description: Bug evidence signals. Required when classification.type.value is 'type/bug'.

mentionedVersions.description: SkiaSharp versions mentioned in the issue.

workedIn.description: Version the reporter claims worked (their words). See regression.workedInVersion for analytical conclusion.

brokeIn.description: Version the reporter claims broke (their words). See regression.brokeInVersion for analytical conclusion.

currentRelevance.description: Whether the issue is still relevant to the current/latest version.

isRegression.description: Analytical conclusion: whether this is a regression based on evidence review. See bugSignals.regressionClaimed for the reporter claim.

reason.description: Why this is or isn't considered a regression.

workedInVersion.description: Last version where this worked, based on analysis. See versionAnalysis.workedIn for reporter claim.

brokeInVersion.description: First version where this broke, based on analysis. See versionAnalysis.brokeIn for reporter claim.

regression.description: Regression analysis. Omit if no regression signals.

likelyFixed.description: Whether the issue appears to be already fixed.

confidence.description: Confidence in the fix status assessment.

reason.description: Why the issue is or isn't considered fixed.

relatedPRs.description: PR numbers that may have fixed this.

relatedCommits.description: Commit SHAs that may have fixed this.

fixedInVersion.description: Version the fix shipped in, if known.

fixStatus.description: Whether the issue may already be fixed. Omit if no fix signals.

analysis.description: Analytical assessment: root cause hypothesis, code investigation, and resolution proposals.

summary.description: Root cause hypothesis and technical analysis. Why this happens, what code paths are involved, what the fix likely requires. Distinct from top-level summary which is a factual restatement.

rationale.description: Single paragraph explaining key classification decisions (replaces per-field rationales).

source.description: Where this came from, e.g. 'issue body', 'comment #3', 'code search'.

interpretation.description: What this signal means for triage.

items.description: A single evidence quote that drove a triage decision.

keySignals.description: Structured evidence quotes that drove triage decisions. Enables downstream querying.

finding.description: What was found at this location.

relevance.description: How relevant this is to the issue. direct=primary code involved. related=secondary/supporting code. context=background information only.

items.description: A code investigation finding with file and line reference.

codeInvestigation.description: Source code investigation results with file:line citations. HIGH VALUE for downstream pipeline steps.

errorFingerprint.description: Advisory only — a short normalized string summarizing the error signature (e.g. 'DllNotFoundException-libSkiaSharp-iOS'). NOT a stable hash. Used as a human-readable hint for cross-issue dedup, not for mechanical matching.

items.description: An open question for downstream investigation.

nextQuestions.description: Open questions that repro or fix should investigate.

hypothesis.description: Best hypothesis for the root cause.

description.description: What the proposed fix or resolution is.

category.description: What kind of proposal. fix=root cause fix. workaround=temporary mitigation. alternative=different approach. investigation=needs more research.

codeSnippet.description: Code snippet illustrating the proposed fix, if applicable.

validated.description: Whether the proposal code has been validated. untested=not yet checked. yes=passed validation. no=failed validation.

confidence.description: Confidence that this proposal would resolve the issue.

effort.description: Estimated effort to implement. cost/xs=~1 day, isolated, well-understood, minimal context needed. cost/s=~2-3 days, self-contained fix, some familiarity required. cost/m=~1 week, small feature or non-trivial fix, some design thinking. cost/l=~2 weeks, cross-cutting change, real design work, multiple components. cost/xl=over a month, architectural, research-heavy, or high coordination cost.

items.description: A single fix or resolution proposal.

proposals.description: Proposed fixes or resolutions, ordered by recommendation.

recommendedProposal.description: Title of the recommended proposal to try first.

recommendedReason.description: Why this proposal is recommended over others.

resolution.description: Root cause hypothesis and fix proposals.

suggestedAction.description: Recommended next action for the maintainer.

suggestedReproPlatform.description: Platform runner for reproduction. Choose based on classification.platforms: macOS for iOS/macOS/tvOS/watchOS bugs, Windows for WPF/WinUI/UWP bugs, Linux for everything else (including WASM, Android, generic). Used by CI to select the correct runner.

actionability.description: Recommended high-level disposition for the issue.

confidence.description: Confidence that this action is appropriate.

linkedIssue.description: Issue number to link (for link-related/link-duplicate actions).

stateReason.description: Required for close-issue. Maps to GitHub API state_reason.

category.description: Required for convert-to-discussion. GitHub discussion category name.

actions.description: Automatable operations. Simplified: just type + description + relevant data.

output.description: Actionable output: recommended disposition and automatable operations.
